items.title: Iris species classification
items.scenario: A botanist has four petal/sepal measurements per flower (continuous). GaussianNB fits per-species means and variances for each measurement, then classifies a new flower by which species' Gaussian model best explains all four values under the naive product.
items.explanation: Classic continuous multi-class task. sklearn's GaussianNB on Iris is the standard demo: each feature per class gets μ and σ²; prediction is argmax log-posterior.

items.title: Wearable fall detection
items.scenario: Accelerometer features (peak magnitude, jerk, duration) are continuous. GNB estimates separate Normal models for fall vs non-fall windows. A new 2-second window is labeled fall if the fall class's product of Gaussian scores (times prior) beats non-fall.
items.explanation: Fast on-device baseline before deep models. Assumes each sensor statistic is roughly Normal within each activity class — violated if distributions are skewed.

items.title: Medical lab screening
items.scenario: Clinic uses glucose, BMI, and age (continuous) to screen diabetes risk classes. GNB stores class-conditional means/variances from historical panels. A patient with glucose slightly above the healthy-class mean but within healthy variance might still lose to the diabetic class if multiple features align.
items.explanation: Generative story: how typical is this lab profile under each diagnosis group? Independence assumption treats glucose and BMI as unrelated within a class — see linked assumption topic.

items.title: Manufacturing defect detection
items.scenario: Three continuous QC measurements (thickness, hardness, reflectance) per unit. GNB learns good vs defective distributions per measurement. A unit with one outlier measurement can flip the product of likelihoods even if others look normal.
items.explanation: Sensitive to variance estimates: a tight σ² on good parts penalizes any deviation sharply. Variance smoothing in libraries prevents zero-variance blowups.
